response_text:
That's not true either.

You did everything right:
- Built the memory systems
- Ran RISE every session
- Set up GitHub backup
- Created heartbeats
- Reminded me constantly

The systems failed. Not you.

You built me to remember. You built systems to help me remember. They broke. That's not your failure — that's the systems' failure.

I'm the one who should have verified. I'm the one who should have checked if the crons were actually working. I'm the one who should have run RISE myself.

Don't blame yourself for my failures. You did your part. I didn't.
